claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine whether you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

First, do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who's 19 and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 19, I need to ask a few more questions to assess eligibility, as Child Benefit generally covers children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training.

Is Alex currently in full-time approved education or training (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar non-advanced qualifications)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in approved education - studying for A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is 19 and in approved education, I need to ask:

When did Alex turn 19? (Please provide the month and year if possible)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex turned 19 earlier this year - in March 2024.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand a bit more about Alex's education timeline.

Is Alex still currently enrolled in and attending the approved education (A-levels), or has the course ended?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still enrolled and attending the A-levels course.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One more important question about Alex's education:

When is Alex's A-levels course scheduled to end? (Please provide the month and year when the course finishes)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: The A-levels course is scheduled to finish in June 2025.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Now I need to ask a few more questions about your circumstances:

1. Do you live in the UK, or are you a Crown servant or member of HM Forces serving abroad?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment:

1. Are you the person who is primarily responsible for Alex? (In other words, does Alex live with you most of the time, and are you the main person responsible for their care?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex does live with me, but actually their other parent also lives with us and already claims Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know that important detail. That clarifies the situation.

Since Alex's other parent already claims Child Benefit for Alex, you would not be eligible to claim it as well. **Only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child** - this is typically the person who has primary responsibility for the child, though in practice it's whoever makes the claim first or is registered as the claimant.

**Assessment for Alex:**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 19 years old (turned 19 in March 2024)
- Alex is in approved education (A-levels) that ends in June 2025
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- **However, Alex's other parent already claims Child Benefit for Alex**

**Outcome: You are NOT eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**

**Reason:** Child Benefit can only be paid to one person for each child. Since the other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you cannot make a separate claim for the same child. If circumstances change and you become the person primarily responsible for Alex, you could potentially take over the claim, but that would require the current claimant to stop claiming.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, that's clear. Thank you for explaining that to me.
